name: Indian Mackerel
description: A staple in wet markets, commonly grilled or fried. Recognized for its affordability and rich flavor.
location: Northern Samar, Eastern Samar, Davao Gulf

name: Pufferfish
description: Though potentially toxic, it's locally known and handled carefully in some coastal communities.

name: Tropical Sea Cucumber
description: Harvested in Biliran and Leyte’s coastal areas; exported as a delicacy.

name: Bangus
description: The national fish of the Philippines and the most important cultured species in the country. Pampanga and Bulacan are major producers.
location: Pampanga, Bulacan, Cebu, Bohol, Leyte, Samar

name: Flying Fish
description: Fish with enlarged pectoral fins that allow them to glide above the water’s surface.
location: Eastern Samar, Southern Leyte, Sulu Sea

name: Spanner Crab
description: Occasionally caught in the deeper waters off Eastern Samar. Highly prized in seafood dishes.

name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and other local dishes. Also known as Bolinao or Tiki-Tiki.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: A medium-sized tuna species that's commercially important in the region. Often caught in coastal waters and sold in wet markets. Also known as Skipjack Tuna.

name: Ghost Crab
description: Fast-moving, burrow-dwelling crabs seen at night on sandy beaches.

name: Sea Grapes
description: Edible seaweed served fresh with vinegar, onions, and tomatoes.

name: Squid
description: Caught mainly in Leyte Gulf and Eastern Samar waters, popular grilled or fried.

name: Mangrove Mudskipper
description: Amphibious fish that walks on land using pectoral fins.
location: Mangrove areas in Samar and Leyte

name: Tamban
description: Small, oily, schooling fish that are essential to marine food webs and heavily harvested for commercial use.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: A medium-sized tuna species that’s important to regional fisheries. Known for its strong flavor and is often used in local dishes like sinaing or paksiw.

name: Blue-Spotted Stingray
description: Small stingray with vivid blue spots; often found on sandy bottoms.

name: Spotted Garden Eel
description: Lives in colonies, swaying from burrows in sandy seabeds.

name: Banded Sea Krait
description: Venomous sea snake with black and white bands; non-aggressive.

name: Crown-of-Thorns Starfish
description: Coral-eating starfish with venomous spines; considered destructive to reefs.

name: Feather Star
description: Colorful echinoderm that clings to corals and fans out its arms at night.

name: Blacktip Reef Shark
description: Small reef shark identified by black tips on fins.
location: Coral reefs of Leyte and Samar

name: Blue Sea Star
description: Vibrant blue echinoderm that regenerates lost limbs.

name: Skunk Clownfish
description: Pale orange clownfish with a white stripe along the back.

name: Ringed Pipefish
description: Long, pipe-shaped body with white rings; swims vertically.

name: Peacock Mantis Shrimp
description: Powerful predator known for club-like claws and colorful armor.

name: Dragonet
description: Small and highly colorful fish found near coral sand beds.

name: Zebra Lionfish
description: Dwarf lionfish with ornate fins and venomous spines.
location: Rocky coral outcrops of Northern Samar

name: Trumpetfish
description: Long-bodied fish that hovers vertically near coral heads.

name: Moorish Idol
description: Black, white, and yellow reef fish with trailing fin.

name: Orange Spotted Filefish
description: Coral feeder with orange spots and elongated snout.

name: Fire Urchin
description: Brightly colored urchin with venomous spines.

name: Broadbarred Firefish
description: A venomous lionfish with wide fins and distinct stripes.

name: Chocolate Chip Sea Star
description: Thick-bodied starfish with dark nodules.

name: Sponge Crab
description: Carries living sponges on its back for camouflage.

name: Pink Sea Cucumber
description: Soft-bodied echinoderm that processes organic matter.

name: Mottled Sea Hare
description: Large sea slug that releases purple ink when disturbed.

name: Comb Jelly
description: Transparent, gelatinous organism that glows with bioluminescence.

name: Green Chromis
description: Schooling fish with shimmering green color.

name: Yellow Boxfish
description: Boxy-shaped juvenile with bright yellow body and black spots.

name: Threadfin Trevally
description: Silvery fish with long trailing dorsal and anal fin rays.

name: Goblin Crab
description: Small, reclusive crab with uneven shell shapes.

name: Saddle Butterflyfish
description: Reef fish with a distinctive black saddle marking.

name: Lunate Fairy Wrasse
description: Male displays brilliant pink, purple, and yellow; active during the day.

name: Brittle Star
description: Fast-moving relative of starfish with flexible, slender arms.

name: Nudibranch
description: Soft-bodied, colorful sea slug; varies widely in appearance.
